assertions.expectation: Explains that regional clusters have multiple control plane replicas and the upgrade is rolling
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Regional clusters (like yours) run 3 control plane replicas across zones' and 'GKE upgrades them one at a time (rolling upgrade)'

assertions.expectation: States that regional control plane upgrades have near-zero API server downtime
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'there is no API server downtime during GKE control plane upgrades' and 'API remains available throughout'

assertions.expectation: Contrasts with zonal clusters where there IS a brief API server outage during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention zonal clusters or contrast them with regional clusters regarding API server downtime during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for control plane upgrade (--master flag)
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command 'gcloud container clusters upgrade CLUSTER_NAME --region us-central1 --master --cluster-version 1.30'

assertions.expectation: Mentions expected duration for control plane upgrade (roughly 10-30 minutes)
assertions.reason: Response states 'Duration: 10-15 minutes typically for 1.29 → 1.30' which falls within the expected range

assertions.expectation: Includes a command to verify the control plane version after upgrade
assertions.reason: Response provides 'gcloud container clusters describe CLUSTER_NAME --region us-central1 --format="value(currentMasterVersion)"' to verify control plane version

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking kube-system pods health after upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' in the monitoring section to check pod health after upgrade

assertions.expectation: Advises checking deprecated API usage before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' command to check for deprecated APIs in pre-flight checks
